children.text: The designation of a senior commissioned officer or civilian member of the senior executive service in each military department with the responsibility for administering the policy.

The assignment of a senior commissioned officer with appropriate acquisition experience and qualifications to act as head of contingency contracting during combat operations, post-conflict operations, and contingency operations, who shall report directly to the commander of the combatant command in whose area of responsibility the operations occur.

A sourcing approach to contingency contracting that is designed to ensure that each military department is prepared to conduct contingency contracting during combat operations, post-conflict operations, and contingency operations, including stabilization and reconstruction operations involving interagency organizations, if required.

children.label: A
children.text: the use of law, regulations, policies, and directives related to contingency contracting operations;

children.label: B
children.text: the appropriate use of rapid acquisition methods, including the use of exceptions to competition requirements under sections 3201 through 3205 of this title, sealed bidding, letter contracts, indefinite delivery-indefinite quantity task orders, set asides under section 8(a) of the Small Business Act (), undefinitized contract actions, and other tools available to expedite the delivery of goods and services during combat operations or post-conflict operations;15 U.S.C. 637(a)

children.label: C
children.text: the appropriate use of rapid acquisition authority, commanders’ emergency response program funds, and other tools unique to contingency contracting; and

children.label: D
children.text: instruction on the necessity for the prompt transition from the use of rapid acquisition authority to the use of full and open competition and other methods of contracting that maximize transparency in the acquisition process.
Appropriate steps to ensure that training is maintained for such personnel even when they are not deployed in a contingency operation.
Such steps as may be needed to ensure jointness and cross-service coordination in the area of contingency contracting.

children.heading: .—Interagency Plans
children.label: b .—Interagency Plans
children.text: To the extent practicable, the joint policy for contingency contracting required by should be taken into account in the development of interagency plans for stabilization and reconstruction operations, consistent with the report submitted by the President under section 1035 of the John Warner National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2007 (; ) on interagency operating procedures for the planning and conduct of stabilization and reconstruction operations.section 3151 of this titlePublic Law 109–364120 Stat. 2388
